description_long: This bungalow features a cross-shaped layout with a prominent, multi-gabled roof covered in gray shingles. The exterior walls are clad in dark wood paneling, complemented by white-trimmed windows and doors. The front facade includes a central entrance flanked by symmetrical window arrangements, while the rear and side elevations display additional windows for ample natural light. One side of the building features a small covered porch supported by a single post, adding architectural interest. The roof design is complex, with intersecting gables and a central raised section, giving the structure a distinctive silhouette. The overall appearance is both rustic and modern, suitable for suburban or semi-rural neighborhoods.
description: A cross-shaped, wood-paneled bungalow with a multi-gabled gray shingle roof, white-trimmed windows, and a small covered porch.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance, symmetrical window arrangement, and the intersecting gabled roof.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting two windows and the small covered porch supported by a single post.
description_view_2: Rear view with large windows, stone accent wall, and a clear view of the intersecting rooflines.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with three windows and the extended gable roof, no porch visible.
attribute_window_arrangement: symmetrical front-facing windows with grouped side and rear windows
